_note: Trace B's recall fixture — THE WOW MOMENT. Two extra memories vs Trace A: (a) the retry-budget DM from Feb 18 surfaces because reinforcement made it hot; (b) the triage_history reinforcement event mem_reinforce_* proves the agent has triaged this cluster before. Invariant 2.

memories.text: Stripe webhook flaking, investigating duplicate charge events from cus_** — looks like the retry path hits processCharge twice when our 200 ack is slow.

Postmortem 2024-01-14 — Stripe webhook regression. Root cause: handler does not check idempotency on retried webhook deliveries. Action items: (1) wire lib/idempotency.ts into webhooks/stripe.ts retry branch, (2) ADR-007 governs key format.

ADR-007 — Idempotency keys for Stripe webhooks. We standardize on `${event.id}:${event.type}` as the key; lib/idempotency.ts.has() must be called before any side-effecting processCharge/processRefund.
DM oncall→eng-lead, Feb 18: 'we should add a retry budget on idempotency keys, this'll bite us again — both processCharge AND processRefund use the same hot path and neither is wrapped'.

User triaged idempotency-key incident in webhooks/stripe.ts — reinforced this cluster. Reinforced memory_ids: [mem_slk_jan14_thread_001, mem_ntn_pm_2024_01_14, mem_ntn_adr_007]. Triage outcome: suspected_fix at webhooks/stripe.ts:84 (idempotency check missing on retry).
